description: Read/write Standard Catalog volume files, browse catalog metadata, and trigger KB ingestion. File IO + catalog browsing delegate to the shared aidp_io URI contract.

tools.description:
Reads or lists files in an AIDP Standard Catalog volume.

When to use: Use when the user asks to fetch, open, read, download, or list a file/document/PDF/dataset stored in a catalog volume. Supports `get` and `list` operations on `master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>` URIs. For workspace files use WorkspaceFileTool; to write use VolumeWriteTool.

How to discover inputs: If the user hasn't named a catalog/schema/volume, FIRST call CatalogBrowserTool(list='volumes', catalog_key=..., schema_key=...) and ask which to use. Default to `operation='list'` first when the target file path is unknown.

How to communicate: Summarize listings as bulleted names; for binary content describe size + MIME type, never dump raw bytes.

Example: CatalogBrowserTool(list='volumes', catalog_key='construction_catalog', schema_key='construction_schema') -> CatalogFileTool(operation='list', source_uri='master:construction_catalog.construction_schema.construction_documents:/') -> CatalogFileTool(operation='get', source_uri='master:construction_catalog.construction_schema.construction_documents:/safety_manual.pdf').

schema.name: operation
schema.description: The action to perform. Use `list` to browse a folder, `get` to download a file. Default to `list` first when the target path is unknown, then call `get` with the chosen path. Example: `list`.

schema.name: source_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified AIDP URI to read from. Format: `master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>` for catalog volumes, or `workspace:/<path>` for workspace files. PREFER this unified URI over the legacy volume_key/catalog/schema/volume/path fields. Required unless you supply volume_key + path or the catalog/schema/volume triple + path.

schema.name: path
schema.description: File path within the volume for `get` (e.g. `/safety_manual.pdf`) or folder path for `list` (default `/`). Ignored when `source_uri` is set. Optional if `source_uri` is provided. Example: `/reports/q3_summary.md`.

schema.name: volume_key
schema.description: Internal UUID identifying the catalog volume. Optional if `source_uri` is provided (preferred) or if catalog/schema/volume names are given instead. Discover keys with CatalogBrowserTool(list='volumes').

schema.name: catalog
schema.description: Catalog name (or key) when resolving by name. Optional if `source_uri` or `volume_key` is supplied — prefer the unified URI form. Example: `construction_catalog`.

schema.name: schema
schema.description: Schema name (or key) within the catalog. Optional if `source_uri` or `volume_key` is supplied — prefer the unified URI form. Example: `construction_schema`.

schema.name: volume
schema.description: Volume name (or key) within the schema. Optional if `source_uri` or `volume_key` is supplied — prefer the unified URI form. Example: `construction_documents`.

_confDescriptions.region: OCI region. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. (e.g. us-ashburn-1)
_confDescriptions.data_lake_ocid: AIDP Data Lake OCID. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. AIDP Console -> top-right -> Workspace info.
_confDescriptions.catalog: Standard catalog NAME. AIDP Console -> Catalogs -> Standard catalogs. Leave blank if using volume_key.
_confDescriptions.schema: Schema NAME within the catalog. AIDP Console -> Catalogs -> <catalog> -> Schemas. Leave blank if using volume_key.
_confDescriptions.volume: Volume NAME within the schema. AIDP Console -> Catalogs -> <catalog> -> <schema> -> Volumes. Leave blank if using volume_key.
_confDescriptions.volume_key: Volume UUID. AIDP Console -> Volumes -> click -> URL contains the key. Alternative to catalog/schema/volume names.
_confDescriptions.max_bytes: Cap on response/file bytes; returns truncated:true when hit.
_confDescriptions.auth_mode: Auth flavor: resource_principal (default, on-cluster), user_principal, or instance_principal.
_confDescriptions.fingerprint: OCI API key fingerprint. Auto-filled from ~/.oci/config.
_confDescriptions.private_key_content: (secret) PEM private key — DO NOT commit. Provide via secret store at deploy time.
_confDescriptions.pass_phrase: (secret) Private key passphrase — DO NOT commit.
_confDescriptions.service_path: Auto-derived from api_version (20240831->dataLakes, 20260430->aiDataPlatforms). Only set if AIDP exposes a custom path.

_schemaDescriptions.source_uri: AIDP URI form: master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path> for catalog volumes, or workspace:/<path> for workspace files. When set, takes precedence over the legacy fields below. Delegated to the shared aidp_io module.

tools.description:
Writes (uploads) a file into an AIDP Standard Catalog volume.

When to use: Use when the user asks to save, persist, upload, store, or write an agent-generated artifact (report, dataset, summary, JSON, markdown) back into a catalog volume. Accepts `master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>` URIs. For workspace writes use WorkspaceFileTool; to read existing files use CatalogFileTool.

How to discover inputs: If the user hasn't named a target volume, FIRST call CatalogBrowserTool(list='volumes', catalog_key=..., schema_key=...) and ask which to use.

How to communicate: Confirm the written URI and byte size in one sentence; do not echo the file content back.

Example: CatalogBrowserTool(list='volumes', catalog_key='finance_catalog', schema_key='reporting_schema') -> VolumeWriteTool(dest_uri='master:finance_catalog.reporting_schema.quarterly_reports:/q3_summary.md', content='# Q3 Summary...').

schema.name: dest_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified destination URI for the write. Format: `master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>`. PREFER this unified URI over the legacy volume_key/catalog/schema/volume/path fields. Workspace writes go through WorkspaceFileTool instead. Required unless you supply volume_key + path or the catalog/schema/volume triple + path.

schema.name: path
schema.description: Destination file path within the volume (e.g. `/reports/summary.md`). Ignored when `dest_uri` is set. Optional if `dest_uri` is provided.

schema.name: content
schema.description: The file body to write. Plain text or stringified JSON/markdown. Required for every write. Example: `# Quarterly Summary\n\nTotal revenue...`.

schema.name: volume_key
schema.description: Internal UUID identifying the catalog volume. Optional if `dest_uri` is provided (preferred) or if catalog/schema/volume names are given instead. Discover keys with CatalogBrowserTool(list='volumes').

schema.name: catalog
schema.description: Catalog name (or key) when resolving by name. Optional if `dest_uri` or `volume_key` is supplied — prefer the unified URI form. Example: `finance_catalog`.

schema.name: schema
schema.description: Schema name (or key) within the catalog. Optional if `dest_uri` or `volume_key` is supplied — prefer the unified URI form. Example: `reporting_schema`.

schema.name: volume
schema.description: Volume name (or key) within the schema. Optional if `dest_uri` or `volume_key` is supplied — prefer the unified URI form. Example: `quarterly_reports`.

_confDescriptions.region: OCI region. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. (e.g. us-ashburn-1)
_confDescriptions.data_lake_ocid: AIDP Data Lake OCID. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. AIDP Console -> top-right -> Workspace info.
_confDescriptions.catalog: Standard catalog NAME. AIDP Console -> Catalogs -> Standard catalogs. Leave blank if using volume_key.
_confDescriptions.schema: Schema NAME within the catalog. AIDP Console -> Catalogs -> <catalog> -> Schemas. Leave blank if using volume_key.
_confDescriptions.volume: Volume NAME within the schema. AIDP Console -> Catalogs -> <catalog> -> <schema> -> Volumes. Leave blank if using volume_key.
_confDescriptions.volume_key: Volume UUID. AIDP Console -> Volumes -> click -> URL contains the key. Alternative to catalog/schema/volume names.
_confDescriptions.auth_mode: Auth flavor: resource_principal (default, on-cluster), user_principal, or instance_principal.
_confDescriptions.fingerprint: OCI API key fingerprint. Auto-filled from ~/.oci/config.
_confDescriptions.private_key_content: (secret) PEM private key — DO NOT commit. Provide via secret store at deploy time.
_confDescriptions.pass_phrase: (secret) Private key passphrase — DO NOT commit.
_confDescriptions.service_path: Auto-derived from api_version (20240831->dataLakes, 20260430->aiDataPlatforms). Only set if AIDP exposes a custom path.

_schemaDescriptions.dest_uri: AIDP master URI form: master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>. When set, supersedes the legacy volume_key / catalog / schema / volume / path fields. Workspace writes are not supported here — use the Workspace File tool. Delegated to the shared aidp_io module.

tools.description:
Discovers AIDP catalog metadata: lists catalogs, schemas, tables, volumes, knowledge bases, or describes a table's columns.

When to use: Use FIRST whenever the user mentions data assets without naming exact keys, or when another tool (CatalogFileTool, VolumeWriteTool, SqlExecTool, KBIngestTool) needs a catalog_key/schema_key/table_key/volume name you don't have. Default to listing before acting.

How to communicate: After listing, summarize the names in a short bulleted list and ask the user which to pick. For `list='table'`, return column names + types.

Example: CatalogBrowserTool(list='catalogs') -> ask user -> CatalogBrowserTool(list='schemas', catalog_key='construction_catalog') -> CatalogBrowserTool(list='volumes', catalog_key='construction_catalog', schema_key='construction_schema') -> hand the chosen volume to CatalogFileTool.

schema.name: list
schema.description: What to discover. Use `catalogs` to enumerate top-level catalogs, `schemas` within a catalog, `tables`/`volumes`/`knowledgeBases` within a schema, or `table` to describe one table's columns. Start with `catalogs` when nothing is known.

schema.name: catalog_key
schema.description: Catalog key identifying the parent catalog. Required for `schemas`, `tables`, `volumes`, `knowledgeBases`, and `table`. Discover with `list='catalogs'` first. Example: `construction_catalog`.

schema.name: schema_key
schema.description: Schema key within the catalog. Required for `tables`, `volumes`, `knowledgeBases`, and `table`. Discover with `list='schemas'` first. Example: `construction_schema`.

schema.name: table_key
schema.description: Table key to describe. Required only when `list='table'`. Discover with `list='tables'` first. Example: `incident_reports`.

_confDescriptions.region: OCI region. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. (e.g. us-ashburn-1)
_confDescriptions.data_lake_ocid: AIDP Data Lake OCID. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. AIDP Console -> top-right -> Workspace info.
_confDescriptions.auth_mode: Auth flavor: resource_principal (default, on-cluster), user_principal, or instance_principal.
_confDescriptions.fingerprint: OCI API key fingerprint. Auto-filled from ~/.oci/config.
_confDescriptions.private_key_content: (secret) PEM private key — DO NOT commit. Provide via secret store at deploy time.
_confDescriptions.pass_phrase: (secret) Private key passphrase — DO NOT commit.
_confDescriptions.service_path: Auto-derived from api_version (20240831->dataLakes, 20260430->aiDataPlatforms). Only set if AIDP exposes a custom path.

tools.description:
Lists ingestion jobs for an AIDP knowledge base, or triggers an ingestion run to (re)index it.

When to use: Use when the user asks to refresh/reindex/re-ingest a knowledge base, check ingestion status, or kick off a new RAG ingest after documents change. Supports `list_jobs` (discovery) and `trigger` (start a run). Default to `list_jobs` first when the job key is unknown.

How to discover inputs: If the user hasn't named a KB, call CatalogBrowserTool(list='knowledgeBases', catalog_key=..., schema_key=...) to enumerate KBs. Then call KBIngestTool(operation='list_jobs', kb_key=...) and ask which job to trigger.

How to communicate: For listings, show job names + last-run status. For triggers, return the run id and current state.

Example: KBIngestTool(operation='list_jobs', kb_key='construction_catalog.construction_schema.safety_kb') -> KBIngestTool(operation='trigger', kb_key='...', job_key='daily_refresh').

schema.name: operation
schema.description: The action to perform. Use `list_jobs` to enumerate ingestion jobs for a KB, or `trigger` to start a run. Default to `list_jobs` first when the job_key is unknown.

schema.name: kb_key
schema.description: Knowledge base key in `catalog.schema.kbName` form. Required for both operations. Discover with CatalogBrowserTool(list='knowledgeBases'). Example: `construction_catalog.construction_schema.safety_kb`.

schema.name: job_key
schema.description: Job key identifying the ingestion job to trigger. Required when `operation='trigger'`; ignored for `list_jobs`. Discover by calling this tool with `operation='list_jobs'` first. Example: `daily_refresh`.

schema.name: run_config
schema.description: Optional run-config body passed through as JSON for `operation='trigger'`. Use to override defaults like document filters or chunk size. Omit to use the job's saved config. Example: `{"chunk_size": 1024}`.

_confDescriptions.region: OCI region. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. (e.g. us-ashburn-1)
_confDescriptions.data_lake_ocid: AIDP Data Lake OCID. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. AIDP Console -> top-right -> Workspace info.
_confDescriptions.kb_key: Knowledge Base key (catalog.schema.kbName). AIDP Console -> Knowledge Bases -> URL.
_confDescriptions.auth_mode: Auth flavor: resource_principal (default, on-cluster), user_principal, or instance_principal.
_confDescriptions.fingerprint: OCI API key fingerprint. Auto-filled from ~/.oci/config.
_confDescriptions.private_key_content: (secret) PEM private key — DO NOT commit. Provide via secret store at deploy time.
_confDescriptions.pass_phrase: (secret) Private key passphrase — DO NOT commit.
_confDescriptions.service_path: Auto-derived from api_version (20240831->dataLakes, 20260430->aiDataPlatforms). Only set if AIDP exposes a custom path.

tools.description:
Reads or lists files in the AIDP workspace (notebooks, scripts, configs under /Workspace).

When to use: Use when the user asks to read, open, list, or browse a workspace file (.ipynb, .py, .md, .txt, config, log) that lives under /Workspace rather than in a catalog volume. For catalog-volume files use CatalogFileTool; for catalog writes use VolumeWriteTool. Accepts `workspace:/<path>` URIs only. Default to `operation='list'` first when the path is unknown.

How to communicate: For listings, show file names as bullets and ask which to open. For `get`, summarize content in plain prose; for binary, describe size + type.

Example: WorkspaceFileTool(operation='list', source_uri='workspace:/Notebooks/') -> WorkspaceFileTool(operation='get', source_uri='workspace:/Notebooks/analysis.ipynb').

schema.name: operation
schema.description: The action to perform. Use `list` to browse a folder, `get` to download a file. Default to `list` first when the target path is unknown.

schema.name: source_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified workspace URI to read from. Format: `workspace:/<path>` only. Master/catalog URIs are not accepted — use CatalogFileTool for those. PREFER this unified URI over the legacy `path` field. Optional if `path` is provided.

schema.name: dest_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified workspace destination URI (reserved for future workspace-write operations). Format: `workspace:/<path>` only. Master/catalog URIs are not accepted — use VolumeWriteTool for those. Optional; currently unused for `get`/`list`.

schema.name: path
schema.description: Workspace path relative to `/Workspace` for `get` (e.g. `Notebooks/foo.ipynb`) or a folder for `list` (default `/`). Ignored when `source_uri`/`dest_uri` is set. Optional if a URI is provided.

_confDescriptions.region: OCI region. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. (e.g. us-ashburn-1)
_confDescriptions.data_lake_ocid: AIDP Data Lake OCID. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json. AIDP Console -> top-right -> Workspace info.
_confDescriptions.workspace_id: AIDP Workspace UUID. Auto-filled from aidp-deploy.config.json.
_confDescriptions.max_bytes: Cap on response/file bytes; returns truncated:true when hit.
_confDescriptions.auth_mode: Auth flavor: resource_principal (default, on-cluster), user_principal, or instance_principal.
_confDescriptions.fingerprint: OCI API key fingerprint. Auto-filled from ~/.oci/config.
_confDescriptions.private_key_content: (secret) PEM private key — DO NOT commit. Provide via secret store at deploy time.
_confDescriptions.pass_phrase: (secret) Private key passphrase — DO NOT commit.
_confDescriptions.service_path: Auto-derived from api_version (20240831->dataLakes, 20260430->aiDataPlatforms). Only set if AIDP exposes a custom path.

_schemaDescriptions.source_uri: AIDP workspace URI form: workspace:/<path>. When set, supersedes the legacy path field. Delegated to the shared aidp_io module.
_schemaDescriptions.dest_uri: AIDP workspace URI form: workspace:/<path>. Reserved for future workspace-write operations through this tool. Delegated to the shared aidp_io module.
